MoveInHierarchy Manual
Version: 1.0
Last updated: 2025-11-19
Asset: Multitool / MoveInHierarchy (Unity Editor Tool)
Target Unity: 2021.3+ (tested with URP templates)

================================================================================
TABLE OF CONTENTS
================================================================================

1. Overview
2. Requirements
3. Installation
4. Quick Start
5. Features Overview
6. Step-by-Step Setup Tutorial
7. Hotkeys
8. Advanced Features
9. Troubleshooting
10. FAQ
11. Uninstall
12. Changelog
13. Support

================================================================================
1. OVERVIEW
================================================================================

This is a simple and lightweight tool that allows you to change the order of objects
within the hierarchy. It supports multi-selection of objects, but only under a single
parent. You can use it with hotkeys or with the compact overlay menu.

================================================================================
2. REQUIREMENTS
================================================================================

- Unity 2021.3 LTS or newer
- Objects in hierarchy (works with any GameObject hierarchy)

================================================================================
3. INSTALLATION
================================================================================

1) Import the package into your project
2) Ensure the folder appears at: Assets/Plugins/Multitool/MoveInHierarchy
3) Open the window: Tools  > Multitool > Move In Hierarchy

================================================================================
4. QUICK START
================================================================================

1) Select one or more objects in the Hierarchy window
2) Use hotkeys to move them:
   - Ctrl+[ to move up one position
   - Ctrl+] to move down one position
   - Ctrl+Shift+[ to move to the top
   - Ctrl+Shift+] to move to the bottom
3) Or use the compact overlay menu in Scene View (toggle via Tools > Multitool > Move In Hierarchy > Show Overlay)

Note: When selecting multiple objects, they must all share the same parent. You cannot select a parent and its child together.

================================================================================
5. FEATURES OVERVIEW
================================================================================

- Move objects up/down in hierarchy: Change the order of objects within the same parent
- Move to top/bottom: Quickly move selected objects to the first or last position
- Multi-selection support: Move multiple objects at once (must share the same parent)
- Hotkey support: Fast keyboard shortcuts for all operations
- Overlay menu: Compact floating menu in Scene View
- Undo/Redo support: All operations support Unity's undo system
- Works in Hierarchy and Scene views: Hotkeys work regardless of which window is focused
- Automatic focus: Hierarchy window automatically scrolls to show moved objects

================================================================================
6. STEP-BY-STEP SETUP TUTORIAL
================================================================================

Step 1: Installation
- Import the MoveInHierarchy package into your Unity project
- Verify the folder is located at: Assets/Plugins/Multitool/MoveInHierarchy

Step 2: Basic Usage
- Open your scene in Unity
- Select an object in the Hierarchy window
- Press Ctrl+[ to move it up, or Ctrl+] to move it down
- The object's position in the hierarchy will change immediately

Step 3: Enabling Overlay Menu
- Go to Tools > Multitool > Move In Hierarchy > Show Overlay
- A compact floating menu will appear in the Scene View
- Click the buttons to move selected objects

Step 4: Multi-Selection
- Hold Ctrl (or Cmd on Mac) and click multiple objects
- Ensure all selected objects share the same parent
- Use any movement method to move the entire group together

Step 5: Undo Operations
- After moving objects, press Ctrl+Z (Cmd+Z on Mac) to undo
- Press Ctrl+Y (Cmd+Shift+Z on Mac) to redo
- All operations are fully integrated with Unity's undo system

================================================================================
7. HOTKEYS
================================================================================

All hotkeys work when objects are selected in the Hierarchy or Scene view:

Ctrl+[ (Cmd+[ on Mac)
- Move selected object(s) up by one position
- Swaps position with the object directly above

Ctrl+] (Cmd+] on Mac)
- Move selected object(s) down by one position
- Swaps position with the object directly below

Ctrl+Shift+[ (Cmd+Shift+[ on Mac)
- Move selected object(s) to the top
- Places them at the first position under their parent

Ctrl+Shift+] (Cmd+Shift+] on Mac)
- Move selected object(s) to the bottom
- Places them at the last position under their parent
Note: All these hotkeys are configurable via Unity's Shortcut Manager
(Edit > Shortcuts). Search for "Multitool/Move In Hierarchy" to find them.

Hotkeys only work when:
- At least one object is selected
- All selected objects share the same parent (for multi-selection)
- No parent and child are selected together

================================================================================
8. ADVANCED FEATURES
================================================================================

Multi-Selection Behavior:
- When multiple objects are selected, they move as a group while maintaining their relative order
- The group moves together, preserving the internal arrangement of selected objects
- Useful for reorganizing entire sections of your hierarchy at once

Overlay Menu:
- Toggle visibility via Tools > Multitool > Move In Hierarchy > Show Overlay
- Appears as a compact floating panel in Scene View
- Buttons are automatically disabled when selection is invalid
- Provides visual feedback with icons for each operation

Parent Grouping:
- Objects are automatically grouped by their parent when moving
- Each parent's children are moved independently
- This allows moving objects from different parents simultaneously (if valid)

Focus Management:
- After moving objects, the Hierarchy window automatically scrolls to show them
- For "Move To Top" and "Move Up", focus goes to the topmost object
- For "Move To Bottom" and "Move Down", focus goes to the bottommost object
- Helps you quickly see the results of your operations

Undo Integration:
- All operations create proper undo entries
- Operations are grouped together for cleaner undo history
- Works seamlessly with Unity's standard undo/redo system
- Each operation type has a descriptive undo name

================================================================================
9. TROUBLESHOOTING
================================================================================

Problem: Hotkeys don't work
Solution: Ensure at least one object is selected in the Hierarchy or Scene view. 
Hotkeys only work when objects are selected and the selection is valid
(all objects share the same parent, no parent-child relationships in selection).

Problem: Multi-selection operations not working
Solution: All selected objects must share the same parent. You cannot select a
parent and its child together. Try selecting only siblings
(objects with the same parent).

Problem: Overlay menu not visible
Solution: Enable it via Tools > Multitool > Move In Hierarchy > Show Overlay.
The menu appears in Scene View as a compact floating panel.

Problem: Objects don't move when using hotkeys
Solution: Check that you're using the correct key combinations (Ctrl+[ or Ctrl+]
on Windows/Linux, Cmd+[ or Cmd+] on Mac). Ensure no other application
is intercepting these keys. Try clicking in the Hierarchy window first
to ensure it has focus.

Problem: Overlay menu buttons are disabled
Solution: This indicates an invalid selection. Ensure all selected objects share
the same parent and no parent-child relationships exist in the selection.

Problem: Undo doesn't work
Solution: All operations support Unity's undo system. If undo isn't working,
check that you haven't disabled Unity's undo system. Use Ctrl+Z (Cmd+Z on Mac)
to undo operations.

Problem: Objects move but hierarchy doesn't scroll to show them
Solution: The tool automatically scrolls the Hierarchy window to show moved
objects. If this doesn't happen, try manually scrolling or clicking on the
moved object to focus it.

Problem: Can't move objects to top/bottom
Solution: If objects are already at the top or bottom of their parent's children
list, they cannot move further. Check the current position in the Hierarchy
window.

Problem: Hotkeys conflict with other Unity shortcuts
Solution: This tool uses standard Unity key combinations. If conflicts occur,
you may need to disable conflicting shortcuts in Unity's Shortcut Manager
(Edit > Shortcuts).

================================================================================
10. FAQ
================================================================================

Q: Does it work with prefabs?
A: Yes, you can move objects in prefab instances. The changes will be reflected
in the prefab instance, but be aware of prefab override behavior in Unity.

Q: Does it affect build performance?
A: No, this is an editor-only tool with no runtime code. It has zero impact on
build size or runtime performance.

Q: Can I change the hotkeys?
A: Yes. Hotkeys are exposed to Unity's Shortcut Manager. Open Edit > Shortcuts,
search for "Multitool/Move In Hierarchy" and assign any key combinations you like.
By default, the tool uses Ctrl+[ / Ctrl+] (Cmd+[ / Cmd+] on Mac) for up/down and
Ctrl+Shift+[ / Ctrl+Shift+] (Cmd+Shift+[ / Cmd+Shift+] on Mac) for top/bottom.

Q: Does it work with UI Toolkit?
A: This tool works with any GameObject hierarchy in Unity, including UI Toolkit
GameObjects. However, it only affects the hierarchy order, not UI Toolkit-specific functionality.

Q: Can I disable the overlay menu?
A: Yes, toggle it off via Tools > Multitool > Move In Hierarchy > Show Overlay.
The setting is saved in EditorPrefs and persists between Unity sessions.

Q: What happens if I select objects from different parents?
A: The tool will only move objects that share the same parent. Objects from
different parents will be grouped by their parent and moved independently within their respective groups.

Q: Can I move objects between different parents?
A: No, this tool only changes the order of objects within the same parent.
To move objects to a different parent, use Unity's standard drag-and-drop in the Hierarchy window.

Q: Does it work with nested hierarchies?
A: Yes, you can move objects at any level of nesting. Just ensure all selected
objects share the same immediate parent.

Q: Will it work if I have a parent and child selected together?
A: No, the tool prevents this to avoid circular dependencies and confusion.
You'll see a notification explaining that the operation is unavailable.

Q: Can I use it with locked GameObjects?
A: The tool respects Unity's standard GameObject locking. Locked objects cannot
be moved, but you can still move their siblings.

Q: Does it work in Play mode?
A: The tool is designed for editor use. While it may work in Play mode, it's
recommended to use it only in Edit mode to avoid unexpected behavior.

Q: What Unity versions are supported?
A: Unity 2021.3 LTS or newer. The tool has been tested with URP templates,
but should work with any Unity project setup.

================================================================================
11. UNINSTALL
================================================================================

1) Close Unity Editor
2) Delete the folder: Assets/Plugins/Multitool/MoveInHierarchy
3) No runtime code remains in builds
4) EditorPrefs settings can be manually cleared if desired (key: MIH_OverlayEnabled)

================================================================================
12. CHANGELOG
================================================================================

Version 1.0.1 (21.11.2025)
- overlay window width fix for unity6

Version 1.0 (2025-09-30):

================================================================================
13. SUPPORT
================================================================================

For support and bug reports:
- Use the Asset Store support link associated with the package
- Include Unity version, reproduction steps, and screenshots
- Check the troubleshooting section first

Thank you for using MoveInHierarchy!
